examples: Cystic fibrosis is the commonest fatal hereditary disease.
Cystic fibrosis is the most common fatal genetic disease in the United States.
People with cystic fibrosis, however, are unable to fight off such bacteria, even though their lungs produce normal amounts of the antibiotic.
